name: Lamb w/ Yoghurt, Coconut Milk & Almond Masala (Bad
recipeInstructions:
1. Cube 2 lb of lamb.
2. Drop the saffron threads into a small bowl or cup, add 1/3 of the boiling water, and soak for at least 10 minutes.
3. Pour the saffron and its soaking liquid into a deep bowl, stir in the yoghurt, caraway seeds, and salt. Add the lamb and turn it with a spoon until all pieces are evenly coated.
4. Marinate the lamb at room temperature for about 30 minutes.
5. Combine almonds and the rest of the boiling water in a bowl, soak for 10 minutes, then blend into a smooth paste. Set aside.
6. Heat ghee over moderate heat in a heavy casserole until fairly hot.
7. Add cinnamon, cardamom, and cloves to the pan, stir for a minute or two, then add onions, garlic, and ginger. Fry until onions are soft and golden brown.
8. Remove lamb with a slotted spoon and add it to the casserole. Stir over moderate heat until lamb browns evenly.
9. Stir in marinade, cold water, almond puree, and red pepper. Cook for 10 minutes stirring occasionally.
10. Pour in coconut milk and bring to a boil. Simmer partially covered for 20 minutes or until lamb is tender.
11. Discard cinnamon and cloves before serving. Mound the lamb attractively on a heated platter and pour sauce over it.
